title: hold statements
text: hold statements Specify whether statements remain open after a commit. Note: This excludes statements for Select requests unless the slctenhance option is also used. USYS$ Mnemonic _PARAMS = hold statements : on | off USYS$ Mnemonic _PARAMS = hs : on | off Use Applies to Mnemonic Connector Syntax DB2 connector DB2 USYS$DB2_PARAMS ODBC connector ODx USYS$ODx_PARAMS MS SQL Server connector MSS USYS$MSS_PARAMS MySQL connector MQL USYS$MQL_PARAMS PostgreSQL connector PGS USYS$PGS_PARAMS Description By default, when a commit is performed, the current hit list is truncated, and the statement used to retrieve it is discarded. If hold statements is set on , the current statement remains open, and the complete hit list remains available, so it is possible to fetch the next items in the hit list. Additionally, for the DB2 and MSS drivers, this option also controls whether cursors are closed when a commit or rollback is done. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
